title: Yoga Teacher Certification Mastery: The Complete 200-Hour Training Blueprint
subtitle: For experienced yogis ready to teach — a comprehensive advanced curriculum covering anatomy, sequencing, and the business of teaching
description: This bootcamp-level certification curriculum prepares advanced yoga practitioners to become confident, knowledgeable, and safe yoga teachers. You'll master the anatomy of movement, the art of sequencing, trauma-informed teaching methodologies, and the business skills needed to build a thriving teaching career.
learningOutcomes: By the end of this course, students will be able to design and teach a complete 60-minute yoga class with confident cueing, appropriate modifications, and safe alignment principles.
By the end of this course, students will be able to evaluate a student's posture and movement patterns to identify contraindications and offer appropriate modifications.
By the end of this course, students will be able to apply trauma-informed teaching principles to create an inclusive, psychologically safe class environment.
By the end of this course, students will be able to construct periodised yoga sequences using traditional Vinyasa, Yin, and Restorative frameworks.
By the end of this course, students will be able to develop a personal teaching brand, manage class scheduling, and grow a student base.

modules.title: Foundations of Teaching Yoga: Philosophy and Ethics
modules.description: Before you step onto the teaching mat, you must understand the philosophical and ethical framework that underpins all authentic yoga teaching. This module covers the eight limbs of yoga, teaching ethics, and the critical differences between practitioner knowledge and teacher knowledge.

lessons.title: Patanjali's Eight Limbs in a Teaching Context
lessons.objective: Students will be able to explain each of the eight limbs of yoga and apply them to modern classroom ethics.
keyTopics: Yamas and Niyamas in teaching ethics

lessons.title: Teaching Ethics, Boundaries, and Scope of Practice
lessons.objective: Students will be able to define appropriate teacher-student boundaries and identify situations that exceed a yoga teacher's scope of practice.
keyTopics: scope of practice vs. medical advice

lessons.title: The Difference Between Practising and Teaching
lessons.objective: Students will be able to distinguish between internal practice awareness and the external observational skills required for effective teaching.

quizQuestions.question: A student tells you they have lower back pain and asks if yoga will cure them. What is the most appropriate response?
options: Confidently say yes — yoga always helps back pain
Refer them to a physiotherapist and explain that yoga can complement, but not replace, medical care
Tell them to do less backbends
Ignore it — students know their own bodies
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Refer them to a physiotherapist and explain that yoga can complement, but not replace, medical care
quizQuestions.explanation: Yoga teachers are not healthcare providers. Advising on medical conditions falls outside scope of practice. The ethical response is to acknowledge the issue, encourage professional evaluation, and offer appropriate modifications within class.

quizQuestions.question: A student approaches you for hands-on adjustments in every class and starts messaging you outside of class. Which ethical principle is most relevant?
options: Ahimsa — be kind and always respond
Satya — tell them how you feel
Maintaining professional boundaries to prevent a dual relationship
Tapas — push through the discomfort
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Maintaining professional boundaries to prevent a dual relationship
quizQuestions.explanation: Dual relationships occur when the teacher-student dynamic blends with personal or dependent relationships. This creates ethical risk and can harm the student's progress and wellbeing. Clear, compassionate boundaries protect both parties.

modules.title: Applied Anatomy for Yoga Teachers
modules.description: This is the module that separates mediocre teachers from excellent ones. You will study the musculoskeletal system with direct application to yoga postures, learning to see what's actually happening in a student's body and why.

lessons.title: The Spine: Structure, Function, and Safe Loading
lessons.objective: Students will be able to identify the five spinal regions, describe their natural curves, and explain safe principles for spinal loading in yoga postures.

lessons.title: The Hip Complex and Lower Body Anatomy
lessons.objective: Students will be able to explain hip joint structure, identify the six deep external rotators, and design modifications for students with limited hip mobility.
keyTopics: anterior pelvic tilt in forward folds

lessons.title: Shoulders and Wrists in Weight-Bearing Postures
lessons.objective: Students will be able to assess shoulder girdle mechanics and teach safe shoulder engagement in Downward Dog, Plank, and arm balances.
keyTopics: wrist anatomy and compression vs. traction

lessons.title: Reading Bodies: Postural Assessment in Class
lessons.objective: Students will be able to conduct a standing postural assessment and use findings to inform cueing decisions during class.

quizQuestions.question: A student with anterior pelvic tilt attempts a forward fold and can't reach past their knees. What is the most appropriate anatomically-informed cue?
options: Tell them to push harder — flexibility comes with practice
Offer a strap and cue them to hinge from the hips rather than rounding the spine
Tell them their hips are wrong and they should come back when more flexible
Have them skip forward folds entirely
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Offer a strap and cue them to hinge from the hips rather than rounding the spine
quizQuestions.explanation: Anterior pelvic tilt reduces available hip flexion range. Rounding the spine to compensate loads the lumbar vertebrae. A strap and hip-hinge cue allows a safe, functional fold that works within their current anatomy.

quizQuestions.question: Why is external rotation of the upper arm important in Downward-Facing Dog?
options: It looks more aesthetically correct
It broadens the shoulder blades and protects the rotator cuff by creating space in the glenohumeral joint
It increases wrist loading for strength building
It is a stylistic preference with no anatomical reason
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: It broadens the shoulder blades and protects the rotator cuff by creating space in the glenohumeral joint
quizQuestions.explanation: External rotation of the humerus draws the greater tubercle away from the acromion, reducing impingement risk. Combined with scapular protraction, this positions the shoulder girdle for safe load bearing.

modules.title: The Art of Sequencing
modules.description: Sequencing is the craft of yoga teaching. This module gives you frameworks for designing classes that have a clear arc — warm-up, peak, cool-down — with intelligent anatomical preparation and a satisfying experience for students of mixed levels.

lessons.title: Peak Pose Sequencing: Building to a Climax
lessons.objective: Students will be able to design a 60-minute class that systematically prepares the body for a chosen peak pose.

lessons.title: Designing for Mixed Levels: Modifications and Progressions
lessons.objective: Students will be able to offer three variations of any posture (entry, standard, advanced) to serve all students simultaneously.

lessons.title: Theming and Intention: The Heart of the Class
lessons.objective: Students will be able to weave a philosophical or emotional theme through a class using opening intention, transitional cues, and closing reflection.

quizQuestions.question: You want to teach Eka Pada Rajakapotasana (King Pigeon) as the peak pose. Which preparatory actions should come earlier in the class?
options: Seated forward folds and hamstring stretches only
Hip external rotation, hip flexor opening, thoracic extension, and shoulder preparation
Jumping jacks and burpees for warmth
Immediately attempt the posture — the body will warm up through it
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Hip external rotation, hip flexor opening, thoracic extension, and shoulder preparation
quizQuestions.explanation: King Pigeon requires deep hip external rotation (for the bent front leg), intense hip flexor stretch (for the back leg), and thoracic extension (for the backbend). Preparing each component systematically prevents injury and deepens the posture.

quizQuestions.question: During a 60-minute Vinyasa class, what is the ideal timing structure?
options: 30 min warm-up, 5 min peak, 25 min cool-down
10 min warm-up, 35 min standing sequences, 15 min floor work and Savasana
5 min meditation, 50 min advanced postures, 5 min rest
Equal time for every posture regardless of intensity
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: 10 min warm-up, 35 min standing sequences, 15 min floor work and Savasana
quizQuestions.explanation: This structure follows the traditional energy arc: a gradual warm-up builds heat, the standing sequence delivers the main work, and floor work with Savasana allows integration. Savasana should never be less than 5 minutes.

bonusResources.title: Light on Yoga by B.K.S. Iyengar
bonusResources.description: The definitive reference book for over 200 postures with precise alignment instructions — every yoga teacher should have this on their shelf.

bonusResources.title: The Key Muscles of Yoga by Ray Long
bonusResources.description: Stunning anatomical illustrations of muscles in action during yoga postures, making anatomy genuinely visual and memorable.

bonusResources.title: Yoga International's Anatomy Series
bonusResources.description: A free video series by Jules Mitchell covering yoga biomechanics and the science behind flexibility — essential for evidence-based teaching.

bonusResources.title: Mindbody Software
bonusResources.description: The industry-standard class scheduling and payment platform used by yoga studios worldwide — learn it before you graduate.
